title: Market Report
text:
TIMESTAMP: 2026-04-29 12:30 PDT

📊 Markets — Wednesday, April 29

Markets edged lower ahead of a deeply divided Fed decision, with Intel and AMD surging on semiconductor sector momentum while broader indexes slipped on Iran conflict uncertainty.

📈 US
• S&P 500: $710.84 (-0.12%)
• Nasdaq: $661.07 (+0.54%)
• Dow: $488.32 (-0.63%)

💱 Forex (from CAD)
• USD/CAD: 1.368
• EUR/CAD: 1.597
• CNY/CAD: 0.200

🛢️ Commodities & Crypto
• Gold: $4,544/oz (-1.14%)
• BTC/USD: $75,497 (-1.08%)
• LTC/USD: $54.72 (-1.78%)

🎯 Movers
• INTC: +10.9% — Intel surged to $93.77, its best day in months, as semiconductor rotation accelerated amid AI hardware demand optimism ahead of earnings season.
• AMD: +4.9% — Rallied to $339.17, tracking Intel higher as chip stocks found a bid despite broader weakness in the sector this week.
• NVDA: -1.8% — Continued to slide ahead of competing AI earnings narratives vs TSM, though off session lows of $207.58.

📰
• The Fed held rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% in its most divided decision since 1992; Powell confirmed he will stay on as a Fed governor after his chair term ends.
• Oil prices remained elevated amid the Iran conflict, keeping a lid on risk appetite and dragging the Dow lower.

⚡ A mixed tape: semiconductors revived on Intel/AMD strength but the broader market struggled under the weight of oil uncertainty and a fractured Fed. The Nasdaq managed a modest gain as mega-cap tech held firm ahead of Meta earnings tonight. Risk-on appetite remains fragile heading into a heavy earnings calendar.

title: Market Report
text:
TIMESTAMP: 2026-04-29 07:27 PDT

📊 Markets — Wednesday, April 29

Fed decision day + hyperscaler earnings in focus; oil tops $112 on Hormuz impasse.

📈 US (prev close)
• S&P 500: 7,138.80 (-0.49%)
• Nasdaq: 24,663.80 (-0.90%)
• Dow: 49,141.93 (-0.05%)
• S&P futures +0.1%, Nasdaq futures +0.2% — little changed pre-market

🌏 Asia
• Nikkei 225: CLOSED (holiday) — prior session at 60,537 (+1.38%, record close)
• Hang Seng: +1.20% (late trade)
• CSI 300: 4,810.35 (+1.10%)
• Kospi: 6,690.90 (+0.75%)

💱 Forex (from CAD)
• USD/CAD: 1.3699
• EUR/CAD: 1.6000
• CNY/CAD: 0.2002

🛢️ Commodities & Crypto
• Brent Crude: ~$112/barrel (8th straight gain; Strait of Hormuz crisis)
• WTI Crude: $99.32 (-0.61%)
• Gold: $4,534.84/oz (-1.34%)
• BTC/USD: $76,679 (+0.47%)
• LTC/USD: $56.20 (+0.88%)

🎯 Movers (>2% or significant news)
• INTC: $90.71 (+7.32%) — Continued rally after blowout Q1 earnings; smashed estimates on $13.6B revenue; broke all-time high above 2000 record

📰 News
• Fed decision today (likely Powell's final meeting as Chair) — rates expected unchanged, focus on forward guidance. Durable goods and housing starts data also due.
• Trifecta of Mag-7 earnings after the close: Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta all report tonight. Meta shares called "iffy" into print; AI monetization narrative on the line.
• UAE to exit OPEC May 1 — major blow to cartel as Hormuz tensions persist.
• OpenAI reportedly missed revenue/user targets, weighing on tech sentiment.
• Intel capitalizing on years of foundry investments, now seeing payoff in Q1 beat.

⚡ Fed day + mega-cap earnings create a binary-event setup. Oil's relentless grind on Hormuz risk is the macro wild card. Tech sentiment is fragile after the OpenAI report, but strong results from MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META tonight could flip the narrative. INTC continues its stunning multi-month re-rate on foundry momentum. Gold pulling back as rate expectations firm.

title: Market Report
text:
TIMESTAMP: 2026-04-27 12:30 PDT

📊 Markets — Monday, April 27, 2026

Wall Street treads water ahead of a blockbuster week of Mag-7 earnings, with NVDA surging over 3% and AMD sliding on semiconductor rotation.

📈 US
• S&P 500 (SPY): $715.20 (+0.18%)
• Nasdaq (QQQ): $663.81 (flat)
• Dow (DIA): $492.20 (flat)

🛢️ Commodities & Crypto
• Gold: $4,682/oz (-0.53%)
• BTC/USD: $76,686 (-2.53%)
• LTC/USD: $55.23 (-1.87%)

🎯 Movers
• **NVDA** (+3.54%): Surged to $215.65, leading the Mag-7 higher ahead of this week's earnings reports — bullish signals noted by analysts.
• **AMD** (-3.16%): Shed nearly $11 to $336.82 as semi names saw rotation before earnings season.
• **BABA** (-2.42%): Dipped to $132.53 amid broader risk-off sentiment on Chinese ADRs.

📰
• Mag-7 earnings week kicks off — Google, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, and Apple all report this week; Goldman Sachs reset AAPL forecasts ahead of its print.
• Ray Dalio recommends up to 15% allocation to gold on Iran war uncertainty; gold holds near $4,682 despite today's pullback.

⚡ Mixed session with a cautious tone ahead of the heaviest earnings week of the season. Semis showed sharp divergence (NVDA surging, AMD sliding), crypto pulled back, and gold held elevated on geopolitical uncertainty. All eyes on Mag-7 results for direction.

title: Market Report
text:

📊 Markets — Tuesday, April 28

UAE quits OPEC as Iran conflict rattles energy markets; OpenAI growth miss slams AI-linked shares ahead of US open.

📈 US (prior close)
• S&P 500: 7,115.20 (-0.51%)
• Nasdaq (QQQ): $657.73 (-0.98%)

🌏 Asia (overnight)

• Kospi: 6,641.02 (+0.39%) — another record high

💱 Forex (from CAD)
• USD/CAD: 1.366
• EUR/CAD: 1.601
• CNY/CAD: 0.200

🛢️ Commodities & Crypto
• Brent Crude: ~$111.16/bbl (+2.7%) — UAE exits OPEC amid Strait of Hormuz crisis
• Gold: $4,569/oz (-2.40%) — sharp pullback from recent highs
• BTC/USD: $75,800 (-2.02%)
• LTC/USD: $54.86 (-1.28%)

🎯 Movers
• TSM: $391.96 (-3.22%) — dragged by OpenAI-linked AI spending fears
• INTC: $82.81 (-2.57%) — gave back some of yesterday's 20% post-earnings surge
• AMD: $324.36 (-3.07%) — semiconductor sector weakness on AI demand jitters
• ORCL: -5% premarket — OpenAI reportedly missed user/revenue targets; $300B Stargate deal in focus
• SoftBank: -10% in Tokyo — steepest AI-related selloff on OpenAI growth headwinds

📰 News
• UAE quit OPEC and OPEC+ Tuesday, dealing a historic blow to the cartel as the Iran war, Strait of Hormuz disruptions, and Saudi-UAE rift converge — Brent crude tops $111
• OpenAI growth shortfall report (missed sales & user targets) sinks SoftBank, Oracle, CoreWeave, and pressures US tech futures; S&P 500 futures -0.6% premarket

⚡ Sentiment: Risk-off bias entering today. The UAE/OPEC exit adds immense crude supply uncertainty atop an already-simmering Iran conflict, pressuring equities globally. Tech faces a double headwind: OpenAI growth doubts revive AI capex scrutiny just ahead of mega-cap earnings. Gold's sharp retreat suggests some liquidation/position-squaring. Pre-market futures point to a lower open. Key data today: home prices and consumer confidence. Earnings on deck: Aflac, Alphabet, Amazon, Ford, Meta, Microsoft.

Iran headlines remain the wildcard for energy-linked moves.

title: Market Report
text:
TIMESTAMP: 2026-04-24 12:50 PDT

📊 Markets — Friday, April 24

Semiconductors erupt across the board as Intel's blockbuster earnings ignite a broad AI-chip rally, pushing Nvidia toward its first all-time close in six months.

📈 US
• S&P 500 (SPY): $713.78 (+0.75%)
• Nasdaq (QQQ): $663.54 (+1.86%)
• Dow (DIA): $492.05 (-0.19%)

🛢️ Commodities & Crypto
• Gold: $4,717.64/oz (+0.51%)
• BTC/USD: $77,600 (-0.87%)
• LTC/USD: $56.65 (+0.78%)

🎯 Movers
• INTC: +22.04% — Intel surges after Q1 earnings reveal an AI data center boom, sending the stock to its best day on record after the chipmaker signaled a massive uptick in data-center demand.
• AMD: +13.56% — Upgraded to Buy by Barron's as the biggest beneficiary of Intel's earnings; AMD also gets a boost ahead of its own earnings, with Seeking Alpha calling the setup asymmetric to the upside.
• TSM: +5.18% — Taiwan Semi rides the Intel-fueled semiconductor wave higher as the AI supply-chain thesis tightens.
• NVDA: +4.23% — Nvidia heads for its first record close in six months, lifted by the broader chip sector euphoria and continued AI infrastructure momentum.
• BABA: +3.23% — Alibaba extends its recent run amid renewed China tech optimism.
• META: +2.60% — Meta climbs alongside the tech rally with broad risk-on flows into mega-cap tech.

📰
• Intel's Q1 earnings sparked a historic rally in chip stocks, with the company citing an "AI data center boom" that lifted the entire semiconductor complex and triggered analyst upgrades across the space.
• Charter Communications suffered its worst day on record as concerns over broadband competition and subscriber losses hammered the stock.

⚡ Overall a decisively risk-on session driven by the Intel earnings tsunami. Semiconductors dominated as the engine of the day's gains, pushing the Nasdaq toward a near-2% advance while the Dow lagged marginally. The AI capex narrative remains firmly intact with chip-sector momentum carrying into year-end positioning.
